january.title: New Year Reset
january.description: Start the year with a single daily habit: ten minutes of movement before your first screen. Track streaks in a notebook—consistency beats intensity.

february.title: Kindness Sprint
february.description: Each day, send one specific thank-you or encouragement to someone. Keep it short and genuine; rotate between colleagues, friends, and family.

march.title: Spring Declutter
march.description: Remove one physical item or digital subscription per day. Donate, recycle, or unsubscribe—end the month lighter than you began.

april.title: Learn in Public
april.description: Publish a short note weekly about something you learned at work. One paragraph, one takeaway—build a trail of small insights.

may.title: Walk & Listen
may.description: Take three fifteen-minute walks per week without podcasts or calls. Notice your neighborhood; jot one observation after each walk.

june.title: Summer Skill Block
june.description: Reserve thirty minutes, three times a week, for deliberate practice on one skill. Use a timer; same slot each session if you can.

july.title: Digital Sunset
july.description: Pick a cutoff time after which screens are off for reading or rest. Protect sleep; adjust the time if travel disrupts the routine.

august.title: Cook One New Dish
august.description: Each week, cook one recipe you have never made. Shop once, prep ahead, and photograph the result for your own archive.

september.title: Back-to-Focus
september.description: Use a single to-do list with a hard cap of three big items per day. Finish before adding more; celebrate closing loops.

october.title: Deep Work October
october.description: Block ninety minutes of uninterrupted work twice a week. Close chat, silence phone, and name the single outcome before you start.

november.title: Gratitude Graph
november.description: Each evening, write one sentence about something that went better than expected. Review the list on the last day of the month.
